name: Emu Steak Au Poivre
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the black peppercorns in a press-closure bag. Crush them with a hammer.
Lightly coat both sides of each steak with the crushed peppercorns.
2. Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sauté the steaks until they are rare or medium-rare, about 3-4 minutes per side. Remove the steaks from the pan and set aside, discarding the oil.
3. Return the steaks to the pan. Add 1/3 cup of Cognac and cook over high heat until the Cognac reduces to about 2 tablespoons. Remove the steaks to a serving platter and keep warm.
4. In the same pan, add the heavy cream and cook over high heat until it boils and thickens. Stir constantly during this process.
5. Add the remaining 2 tablespoons of Cognac and salt to taste. Stir until combined.
6. Spoon the sauce over the steaks and serve.
